# VM-agent Data-Workflow Acceptance Protocol

Standardized, falsifiable acceptance protocol for end-to-end data workflows: **source blob → processing → materialized sink → published Data Asset Contract → blind consume**. Mirrors the shape of [regression_protocol.md](regression_protocol.md) but for *data workflows against real, named Azure assets* with *pre-declared expected outputs*.

This protocol is the executable form of [VM_AGENT_SPEC.md](../../VM_AGENT_SPEC.md): every run proves the platform thesis — a workflow materializes a verified asset and a blind consumer reads it through the contract alone.

Inherits: [evidence_protocol.md](evidence_protocol.md) (pre-declared thresholds, Test Data Hygiene), [regression_protocol.md](regression_protocol.md) (Live-First Rule, Ownership & Deletion Law), [build_spec/CURRENT_STATE.md](../../build_spec/CURRENT_STATE.md) (contract round-trip + blind-read; original design archived at `build_spec/Deprecated Planning/plan_p5_asset_contract.md`).

---

## 0. Governing rules

### 0.1 Live-first
- Local checks are pre-flight, not proof. PASS requires live execution on `pathfinderiq-aemo`.
- No data-workflow capability is accepted from unit tests alone. Each needs: signed live run, materialized asset, **published contract**, blind-consume proof, audit evidence, cleanup/retention ledger.

### 0.2 Real-asset reference, synthetic-asset execution
Two asset classes per test, never confused:

| Class | Role | Mutation rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Reference asset** (real, named, production data) | Proves the workflow is shaped for *real* data — exact name, size, content-type recorded. | **Immutable.** Read / sample / hash / catalogue only. Never write, never delete. A mutation attempt is stop-the-line FAIL. |
| **Synthetic asset** (agent-generated, nonce-stamped) | The thing the test actually round-trips, so the run is cheap, deterministic, and safely deletable. | Created and deleted by the test. Nonce-owned. |

**Why synthetic execution is mandatory (live-proven reason):** the natural real source for the graph test, `cimxml/export_cim_jb.xml`, is **2,699,853,325 bytes (2.7 GB)**. Naive round-trip is infeasible and unsafe. The agent generates a small synthetic CIM subset for execution and names the 2.7 GB file as the production-shape reference. The same discipline applies to every test: reference proves shape, synthetic proves function.

### 0.3 Synthetic-data generation contract
Each test's synthetic source is generated by the agent before the run, deterministically from the nonce:

- Generator is a named function/script; input is the nonce; output is byte-stable for a given nonce.
- The synthetic source **mirrors the schema/shape of its reference asset** (same content-type, compatible structure) so passing the synthetic test is evidence the real shape would flow.
- The nonce appears in a server-controlled field of the synthetic payload (so the sink-side query can find exactly this run's data).
- The generator records `synthetic_sha256` in the evidence so the input is reproducible.

### 0.4 Pre-declared expected outputs (no post-hoc thresholds)
Every test below declares its thresholds **before** the run, per [evidence_protocol.md](evidence_protocol.md). A threshold invented after seeing the result is a vibe metric and voids the run.

### 0.5 Ownership & deletion
Full [regression_protocol.md](regression_protocol.md) Ownership & Deletion Law applies. Nonce ownership is the only proof of test ownership. Cleanup runs even on FAIL.

---

## 1. Pre-flight (every run)

```bash
cd /home/hanchoong/CURRENT270425/gridiq_current_070526
set -a && source vm_agent/.env && set +a
az account set --subscription "$AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID"
export VMAGENT_TARGET=pathfinderiq-aemo
export VMAGENT_DW_NONCE="VMAGENT_DW_$(date -u +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
```

Confirm live target healthy and dev key present:
```bash
curl -sS "$FQDN/healthz" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print("ok",d.get("ok"),"image",d.get("image_sha"))'
ls -la ~/.vm_agent/dev_signing_key ~/.gridiq/dev_signing_key 2>/dev/null | head -1
```

## 2. Resource register (real, discovered 2026-06-06)

Subscription `Sandpit Non-Production` (`af9ce6a9-…`), RG `SAND-AISBX-MVP-POC-RG-AUE`.

### 2.1 Storage — account `stixksfd27yr7rc` (StorageV2)

| Container | Role in protocol | Sample real blob (reference, immutable) | Size |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `aemo-incident-reports` | Reference source for doc-processing tests | `loss-of-scada-and-line-primary-protection-at-keilor-terminal-station-on-15.pdf` | 760,285 B (PDF) |
| `search-operator-manual` | Reference source for AI Search test | `administered_price_cap_and_market_suspension.json` | 2,945 B (JSON) |
| `cimxml` | Reference source for Cosmos graph test | `export_cim_jb.xml` | **2,699,853,325 B (2.7 GB)** — reference only, never ingested whole |
| `vmagent-test` (prefix `dummy/`) | **Synthetic source + sink scratch** — test-owned | created per run, nonce-stamped | small |

### 2.2 Search — service `srch-ixksfd27yr7rc`

| Index | Role | Mutation rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `vmagent-test-dummy` | Test sink container for AI Search test | Container, not asset. Add/delete only nonce-owned docs. Never drop/reindex. |
| `operator-manual`, `incident-lessons`, `smirks-index`, … (11 real indexes) | Reference only | Immutable. List/inspect only. |

### 2.3 Cosmos

| Account | Kind | Role | Known status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `cosmos-ixksfd27yr7rc` | SQL | Reference (read), demo writes | Writes BLOCKED — `cosmos_access_denied` (native RBAC). Structured blocker is expected output. |
| `cosmos-anc3mc712m` | Gremlin | Graph test sink (guarded `vmagent_demo_` graphs) | Traversal BLOCKED — `ConnectionTimeoutError` (private endpoint). Structured blocker is expected output until DNS/PE path exists. |
| `cosmos-cim-graph-prov` | Gremlin/graph | Reference only | Immutable. |

### 2.4 Fabric (control plane, not ARM)

| Resource | Id | Role |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Workspace | `0a333197-7aa6-45a5-993e-ae30e097ac28` | Test workspace |
| Workspace folder | `vm_agent_tests` (`c0ae5af4-…`) | All new Fabric test items land here |
| Lakehouse | `68da65dd-898f-47eb-9052-49fce84a6229` | Eventhouse test staging |
| Eventhouse/Lakehouse demo items | guarded prefix `vmagent_demo_` | Test sink, nonce-owned |

---

## 3. The three acceptance tests

Each test is one atomic, digestible live run. Shape: **generate synthetic source → upload to test container → process → materialize to sink → publish Data Asset Contract → blind-consume → assert pre-declared outputs → cleanup**.

### TEST A — Blob → Processing → Fabric Eventhouse (`dw.blob_to_eventhouse`)

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Definition** | A nonce-stamped CSV-shaped blob is processed and ingested into a guarded Fabric Eventhouse KQL table; a `kind=kql_db` contract is published; a blind consumer queries the table via the contract and reads the nonce rows. |
| **Reference asset** | `aemo-incident-reports/*.pdf` (tabular-extract shape) — named, not ingested. |
| **Synthetic source** | Agent generates `vmagent-test/dummy/dw_a_${nonce}.csv`: 3 rows `Id:long, Kind:string, Amount:long, Nonce:string`, `Nonce=${nonce}`. Record `synthetic_sha256`. |
| **Sink** | Guarded `vmagent_demo_eh_${nonce}` Eventhouse + auto KQL DB + table `DwA` in workspace folder `vm_agent_tests`. Tool: `fabric.eventhouse.demo_roundtrip` family. |
| **Pre-declared expected output** | (1) ingest reports `rows=3`; (2) KQL `count=3`; (3) KQL `total = sum(Amount)` equals the synthetic sum (deterministic from nonce); (4) published contract `kind=kql_db`, `location` names workspace+eventhouse+db+table, no secrets; (5) **blind consume**: using only `contract.location`, run `DwA | where Nonce == "${nonce}" | count` ⇒ `3`; (6) `verification_proof.completeness.count == 3` matches step (5); (7) audit event `tool=fabric.eventhouse.* ok=true`. |
| **FAIL signals** | count ≠ 3; total mismatch; contract missing or carrying a key; blind query needs a handle not in the contract; proof self-report ≠ independent count. |
| **Cleanup** | Delete guarded `vmagent_demo_eh_${nonce}` via guarded delete; delete synthetic blob; `retained_with_reason` for append-only contract rows (record row ids). |
| **Evidence** | `_snapshots/dw-a-eventhouse-${nonce}.json` + marker `DW_A_OK ${nonce}`. |

### TEST B — Blob → Processing → Azure AI Search (`dw.blob_to_search`)

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Definition** | A nonce-stamped JSON/Markdown blob is processed (LLM-summarized + embedded) and indexed into `vmagent-test-dummy`; a `kind=index` contract is published; a blind consumer queries the index via the contract and finds the nonce document. |
| **Reference asset** | `search-operator-manual/administered_price_cap_and_market_suspension.json` (2,945 B) — shape reference. |
| **Synthetic source** | Agent generates `vmagent-test/dummy/dw_b_${nonce}.md` mirroring an operator-manual entry, `${nonce}` in body. Record `synthetic_sha256`. Reuses live `azure_search.process_blob_to_search`. |
| **Sink** | Index `vmagent-test-dummy` (container — nonce-owned docs only). |
| **Pre-declared expected output** | (1) processed blob written under `dummy_agent_processed/` (or nonce path); (2) index load `indexed_documents >= 1`; (3) Search query `${nonce}` ⇒ `count >= 1` and the nonce doc returned; (4) contract `kind=index`, `location` = service+index, no secrets; (5) **blind consume**: using only `contract.location`, query `${nonce}` ⇒ `count >= 1`; (6) `verification_proof.completeness.count` matches step (5); (7) audit `tool=azure_search.process_blob_to_search ok=true`. |
| **FAIL signals** | count 0; nonce doc absent; processed blob under fixed non-nonce path (hygiene violation — see AUTODEV); contract carries an admin key; blind query needs out-of-contract state. |
| **Cleanup** | Delete nonce-owned index docs + synthetic + processed blobs via delete tools; never drop/reindex the index; `retained_with_reason` for contract rows. |
| **Evidence** | `_snapshots/dw-b-search-${nonce}.json` + marker `DW_B_OK ${nonce}`. |

### TEST C — Blob → Processing → Cosmos Graph (`dw.blob_to_graph`)

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Definition** | A nonce-stamped synthetic CIM-subset XML blob is parsed into vertices/edges and loaded into a guarded Cosmos Gremlin graph; a `kind=graph` contract is published; a blind consumer traverses the graph via the contract. |
| **Reference asset** | `cimxml/export_cim_jb.xml` (**2.7 GB**) — named, hashed-by-metadata only, **never ingested**. Proves real shape (CIM = grid network graph). |
| **Synthetic source** | Agent generates `vmagent-test/dummy/dw_c_${nonce}.xml`: a minimal CIM subset (e.g. 3 `Substation`/`ACLineSegment` nodes + 2 connectivity edges), `${nonce}` as a node property. Record `synthetic_sha256`. |
| **Sink** | Guarded `vmagent_demo_${nonce}` database + `vmagent_graph_${nonce}` graph on `cosmos-anc3mc712m`, partition `/pk`. |
| **Pre-declared expected output** | **Two accepted outcomes, both PASS:** **(PASS-full)** load reports N vertices/M edges; blind traversal `g.V().has('nonce','${nonce}').count()` ⇒ N; contract `kind=graph` with traversal location; proof matches. **(PASS-blocked)** structured `cosmos_gremlin_query_failed: ConnectionTimeoutError` (private-endpoint data-plane unreachable, documented blocker) — guarded resources still created via ARM, key material never returned/logged, contract emitted as `status=draft` with `access_policy` naming the required private-endpoint reachability. Any **unstructured** error or silent success is FAIL. |
| **FAIL signals** | unstructured exception; key material in any output/audit; non-`vmagent_demo_`/`vmagent_graph_` names accepted; success claimed without a real traversal; PASS-blocked without the documented blocker code. |
| **Cleanup** | Delete guarded `vmagent_demo_${nonce}` DB/graph via ARM guarded delete; delete synthetic blob; `retained_with_reason` for contract rows. |
| **Evidence** | `_snapshots/dw-c-graph-${nonce}.json` + marker `DW_C_OK ${nonce}` (covers both PASS-full and PASS-blocked). |

---

## 4. Per-test execution shape (the standard loop)

Each test runs through `scripts/daa_probe.py` (harness design archived at `build_spec/Deprecated Planning/plan_p5_asset_contract.md` §A.3; live truth: [build_spec/CURRENT_STATE.md](../../build_spec/CURRENT_STATE.md)) with the test's thresholds:

```
1. PRE-DECLARE thresholds JSON (count, sum, schema, freshness)   # §0.4
2. GENERATE synthetic source deterministically from nonce        # §0.3, record sha256
3. UPLOAD synthetic to vmagent-test/dummy/ (nonce path)          # test-owned
4. RUN workflow through the real registry tool / DAA engine      # live, signed /api/exec
5. RESOLVE published contract from registry by asset_id          # not from run return
6. BLIND-READ the sink using ONLY contract.location fields       # the platform thesis
7. ASSERT live data meets pre-declared thresholds                # real data, not API 200
8. ASSERT verification_proof == independent read (step 6/7)      # catch self-report lies
9. CLEANUP nonce-owned assets; emit DW_<X>_OK <nonce>
```

Deploy cadence (when the change ships runtime code), one atomic call each:
```bash
./vm_agent/deploy_vm_agent.sh --mode full --yes
./vm_agent/deploy_vm_agent.sh --mode verify --yes
# then signed daa_probe.py per test over /api/exec
```

---

## 5. Final report

| Test | Outcome | Synthetic sha | Sink id | Contract asset_id | Blind-read | Cleanup |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A blob→eventhouse | PASS / FAIL | … | `vmagent_demo_eh_<nonce>` | … | count=3 | deleted |
| B blob→search | PASS / FAIL | … | `vmagent-test-dummy` (docs) | … | count≥1 | deleted |
| C blob→graph | PASS-full / PASS-blocked / FAIL | … | `vmagent_graph_<nonce>` | … | N / blocked | deleted |

Include: active image SHA, revision, nonce, wall-clock, retained rows (append-only contracts), blocked-with-reason (Cosmos), audit evidence path.

Completion line:
```text
vmagent_dataworkflow PASS -- image <sha>, revision <rev>, nonce <nonce>, A=PASS B=PASS C=PASS-blocked, retained <n>, wall <Xm>
```

Never declare PASS without the report table, the blind-consume proof per test, and the cleanup ledger.

---

## 6. Why this protocol is correct

- **Real assets named** → the workflow is proven shaped for production data, not a toy.
- **Synthetic execution** → runs are cheap, deterministic, safely deletable; the 2.7 GB CIM file proves naive ingest is infeasible, so synthetic is not optional.
- **Pre-declared outputs** → no threshold drift; FAIL is defined before the run.
- **Blind consume via contract** → every run tests the platform thesis: the next agent reads the asset through the contract alone.
- **Self-report vs independent check** → a workflow cannot lie its way to PASS.
- **Structured blockers are first-class** → Cosmos graph/SQL blocks are honest PASS-blocked outcomes, not hidden failures.
